The question is asking how many political lieutenants served specifically in the year 1949. Since each row represents a lieutenant and the "took office" and "left office" columns indicate the years of service, we need to filter for rows where either "took office" or "left office" equals 1949. Since "took office" and "left office" are separate columns, we'll need to examine both to catch any lieutenant who started or ended their service in 1949. In this case, we can see that Georges-Henri Héon both took office and left office in 1949.
Based on the extracted sub-table, Georges-Henri Héon served as political lieutenant in 1949 and left office the same year. This means only one lieutenant served in 1949.
